APPLY ECONOMIC FRAMEWORKS. Azure and other cloud services: demand is structurally supported if AI workloads scale, because Azure revenue is mainly affected by infrastructure-as-a-service and platform-as-a-service consumption, and Microsoft says Azure AI offerings provide supercomputing power and AI cloud services for companies seeking to optimize and scale with AI (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Intelligent Cloud — Server Products and Cloud Services). Complementarity is strong: Satya Nadella described agents as the new apps and listed required services including model catalogs, tuning, orchestration, context engineering, AI safety, observability, and security, all of which pull demand toward cloud platforms (Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks). Substitution risk exists because Azure faces competition from cloud service providers and open-source offerings, and Microsoft’s AI offerings compete with hyperscalers, emerging competitors, and open-source offerings (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Intelligent Cloud — Competition). Supply is both moat and risk: Microsoft states datacenters depend on permitted and buildable land, predictable energy, networking supplies, and servers including GPUs, while Q2 FY2026 Azure demand exceeded available supply and Q2 FY2026 capital expenditures were $37.5 billion with roughly two-thirds on short-lived assets, primarily GPUs and CPUs (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Operations; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud: demand and complementarity are positive if Copilot and agents increase ARPU, because Microsoft says Microsoft 365 Commercial growth depends on reaching new users and adding AI-enabled tools, features, and agentic scenarios, while revenue is affected by installed-base growth and average revenue per user expansion (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Productivity and Business Processes — Microsoft 365 Commercial Products and Cloud Services). In Q2 FY2026, Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 17% in constant currency, ARPU growth was led by E5 and Microsoft 365 Copilot, paid Microsoft 365 Commercial seats grew 6% to over 450 million, and Microsoft 365 Copilot had 15 million paid seats with seat adds up over 160% year over year (Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks; Satya Nadella prepared remarks). Substitution risk is real because Office competitors include software and global application vendors, web-based and mobile application companies, and AI-first application companies (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Productivity and Business Processes — Competition). Supply constraints matter indirectly because growing AI product usage pressured gross margin in Q2 FY2026 and company gross margin percentage was 68%, down slightly year over year, primarily due to AI infrastructure investment and AI product usage (Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Dynamics 365: demand is positive if AI agents embed into business workflows; Microsoft states Dynamics revenue is driven by users licensed, applications consumed, ARPU expansion, and the shift to Dynamics 365, while Q2 FY2026 Dynamics 365 revenue increased 19% in constant currency (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Productivity and Business Processes — Dynamics Products and Cloud Services; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). LinkedIn: demand depends on member engagement and AI-enabled services, and Q2 FY2026 LinkedIn revenue increased 11% in constant currency, led by Marketing Solutions (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, Productivity and Business Processes — LinkedIn; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Server products and enterprise services: this is mixed; FY2025 Server products revenue decreased 3% because of continued customer shift to cloud offerings, but Q2 FY2026 on-premises server revenue increased 21% in constant currency due to hybrid solutions, SQL Server 2025, and transactional purchasing ahead of memory price increases (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Segment Results of Operations — Intelligent Cloud; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Windows and Devices: AI can support demand through the AI PC category and Windows integration, because Microsoft lists growth of the AI PC category as a Windows OEM revenue factor, and Windows 11 surpassed 1 billion users, up over 45% year over year (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, More Personal Computing — Windows and Devices; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Satya Nadella prepared remarks). The headwind is cyclical and supply-chain-linked: Q2 FY2026 Windows OEM and Devices revenue increased 1%, but management guided Windows OEM revenue to decline roughly 10% in Q3 FY2026 and said increased memory pricing could create volatility (Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). Search and news advertising: AI is both complement and substitution risk; Microsoft says Copilot, Edge, and Bing are key tools for user acquisition and engagement, but the business competes with search engines, websites, social platforms, and portals (FY2025 Form 10-K, Part I, Item 1, More Personal Computing — Search and News Advertising; Competition). Gaming: AI diffusion is less directly evidenced in the disclosures; FY2025 Gaming revenue was $23,455 million and Xbox content and services revenue grew 16%, but Q2 FY2026 Gaming revenue decreased 9% in constant currency and Xbox content and services revenue decreased 5% in constant currency because of first-party content weakness (FY2025 Form 10-K, Note 18 — Revenue by significant product and service offerings; FY2025 Form 10-K, Part II, Item 7, Segment Results of Operations — More Personal Computing; Q2 FY2026 earnings call, Amy Hood prepared remarks). 3.
